summary: Phase 2 runs scorer design and methodology docs in parallel, 2-week budget, gated deliverables before Phase 3
context: Replacement for 01KQ0NP1NBSSZNFYAW03SVWTC3, which was truncated by twining_record's NL parser. Written with structured fields directly in the decision JSON.
rationale: Naming research time explicitly as gated-with-deliverable prevents open-loop exploration. The scorer design and methodology documents are themselves the research contribution if the flagship run does not produce a clean effect. Existing scorers are calibrated for 12-session scenarios, not multi-sprint accumulation. LLM-leveraged parallel work is expected to compress wall-clock time below the sequential 4-week estimate.
constraints: 2-week total wall-clock time budget for Phase 2
Methodology document must specify a per-condition-fixture fallback plan before flagship run starts
Scorers must be calibrated for multi-sprint accumulation, not 12-session scenario horizons

alternatives.reason_rejected: Too slow at 4 weeks; no forcing function to compress wall-clock time.

alternatives.option: No time budget, research until done
alternatives.reason_rejected: Open-loop with no forcing function; risks unbounded exploration without committed deliverables.
Calibrated for wrong horizon — existing scorers target 12-session scenarios, not multi-sprint accumulation.
Discovery mid-Phase 4 that twining-default and shared-markdown produce similar sprint-1 states would cost more than a pre-commitment flag stating the per-condition-fixture fallback up front.

assumptions: LLM-leveraged parallel work on the two docs fits within 2 weeks wall-clock
End-of-sprint-1 state differs meaningfully across conditions (required for single-fixture branching to be valid)
Deliverable-gated research produces stronger output than open-loop exploration for this kind of methodology work
